  <h2 id="lecture_heading" class="section-title dsp-flex-xs flex-align-items-center-xs" data-lecture-id="22464200" data-next-lecture-id="23909722" data-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/22464200" data-next-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/23909722" ,="" data-previous-lecture-url="/courses/465756/lectures/32265875" data-previous-lecture-id="32265875">

    🔑 4. No Polaris Star, No Progress
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=2 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=40520969, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-40520969">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><em>The Polaris Star is the brightest star in a constellation.</em></p><p>It is the guiding star.</p><p>When you're lost, it gives you direction.</p><p>You need to have one when you're trying to accomplish anything worthwhile.</p><p>Are you good?</p><p>Are you thriving?</p><p>Or does it sometimes feel like you're on the verge of emotional and creative bankruptcy?  </p><p>Bankruptcy is when you don't have enough resources to meet your needs. </p><p>A sure sign is... You feel overwhelmed.</p><p>Another sign is... You don't know what to do because there's too many options.</p><p>"You've been cursed with too many talents," you'll say.</p><p>"You've been cursed with the inability to choose," I say.</p><p>Maybe it's time to focus?</p><p>The real epidemic facing creatives today is: <em>not being able to decide what they should focus on</em>.</p><p>That's why people have trouble picking their message.</p><p>FOMO.</p><p>And then when they're out in the real world, they can't pick their Polaris Star either.</p><p>If you know your message, then you already know your Polaris Star. RUN WITH IT!</p><p>But if you don't know your message, then you need to at least figure out your Polaris Star.</p><p>People will sometimes say, "Oh, you mean I need to know my goal?"</p><p>No, your Polaris Star is not your "goal".</p><h2>Polaris Star = Your vision for who you will become.</h2><p>And it’s that clarity of vision that GIVES BIRTH to what your goals should be.</p><p>Decision making becomes easy. You know what to say yes to… And what to refuse. </p><p>Knowing your Polaris Star enables you to turn off the noise, ignore the distractions, and ward off the temptation to settle for less. Having your Polaris Star light the way for you is your only chance at developing a resolve that is infinitely sustainable.</p><p>People will say “be authentic”. Or “speak your truth”.</p><p>But how do you know what that is if you have nothing to measure it against?</p><p>When you have a Polaris star you won’t measure "authenticity" and "truth" against your current feelings and moods... You will measure them against the vision of who you truly want to become. </p><p><em>Now, instead of pushing your way through obstacles, you’re pulled forward to your destiny. </em></p><p><strong>Now, instead of being wishy-washy about the future-- it's so clear you can taste it. That's when people will want to attach themselves to your wagon. The world will HELP YOU succeed. But that will only happen when you have a clear vision of where you're headed. If YOU can't see it, neither will we.</strong></p><p><br></p><h1><strong>I keep giving you the solution... And you keep avoiding the truth. </strong></h1><p>I see so many people sawing the very branch that they're sitting on by chasing too many goals at the same time.</p><p>Diversification is a strategy that people use when they don’t know what they’re doing. They thin out their resources because they have FOMO.</p><p>I get it.</p><p>You’re scared because you don’t know what will work. But you'll never know what works until you focus on one thing.</p><p>Yes, even if it fails. </p><p>Find out that it's the "wrong thing" faster. And then switch to the winner. In silicon valley they call it "failing fast".</p><p>Don't be stuck in the middle for the rest of your life... And then regret.</p><p>As a normal human being it’s essential to be well rounded, yes... Complex, yes... Nuanced, yes.</p><p>But being the G.O.A.T. is not being a "normal human being".</p><p>Being the G.O.A.T means throwing out "well-rounded" for now.</p><p>You simplify your existence so that everything revolves around ONE goal. </p><p>You refuse to dilute your efforts.</p><p>Aaron Z emailed me this week:</p><p>"Bronques, I might have 3 messages. Lol. Is that allowed?" </p><p>Aaron has been working hard.</p><p>But everyone needs someone in their corner.</p><p>This was my reply:</p><p>===</p><p>Anything is allowed. There are no rules. But success leaves clues:</p><p>If you want to be one of the people that has an impact on our culture or society at large... Then you'll need a Polaris Star.</p><p>If you're trying to make an impact, narrow down your focus until you've found that ONE thing that you can ride with for the next few years...</p><p>I'm not saying this is the only path.</p><p>It's just the FASTEST path.</p><p>If you want to be the G.O.A.T. ... First be "Michael Jordan the basketball player" before you try your hand at being "Michael Jordan the baseball player". (Yes, that actually happened... But not until he was G.O.A.T -- He gets it.)</p><p>====</p><p>Omar Z answered this when I asked him what his career aspiration was:</p><p>"Writing poetry, vlogging, and making short films."</p><p>Pick a lane, bro.</p><p>And don't try to cheat.</p><p>A Polaris Star isn't a constellation.</p><p>Don't go trying to make what should be ONE GOAL, multi-hyphenated.</p><p><strong>Don’t scatter your forces. </strong></p><p>No, make it so simple that even a child could understand.</p><p>Like this:</p><p>"My goal is to publish a book of poetry by the end of the year."</p><p>Take Rupi Kaur for example. She writes poetry just like Omar Z. She also has other talents just like Omar Z. One of those talents is illustration. She's been drawing since she was 5. If you've ever read interviews with her, you'll know that she cherishes illustration. But being a published poet was her Polaris Star. So her drawings became a tool in service to that goal. She picked her one thing and made it #1. And she succeeded at lightning speed.</p><p>When will you do the same?</p><p>Think about it. Why have you been so afraid of commitment?</p><p>Do you feel like you're gonna miss out on something? </p><p>Or is it because having only one thing seems too boring?</p><p>Or because having laser type focus will mean that you "have no life"?</p><p>The reality I'm trying to convey to you before it's too late is:</p><p><strong>You miss out on EVERYTHING when you DON'T choose.</strong></p><p>When you stay on the surface...</p><p>That's what the normals do.</p><p>Artists...? They go deep.</p><p>Draw your line in the sand. </p><p>Put your stake in the ground.</p><p>You need to change your diet from your current "emotion-du-jour"...</p><p>Into... holding one thought, one goal, one outcome in your mind for the next 9 months.</p><p>Once you penetrate, you can deviate.</p><p>But until then, be a laser beam.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>G.O.A.T. > FOMO.</strong></h1><p>People may appear supportive when they tell you you can “do it all”. And they may very well be good-intentioned... But those people are doing you the most harm.</p><p>The people with your best interest in mind are those who see your strengths, whatever they are and get you to double down on them.</p><p>Every time you try to go astray, they remind you to focus.</p><p>You only get that kind of advice from people who have been there.</p><p>You have to have discipline if you want to achieve your goals.</p><p>But the discipline isn’t "doing what must be done".</p><p>The discipline is ignoring every other thing trying to get a piece of you.</p><p>When Steve Jobs came back to Apple in 1993 (after being kicked out in 1985), the company was on the verge of bankruptcy.</p><p>How did he turn things around?</p><p>Focus. </p><p>He reduced the number of products they were selling from 350 individual products to a lean 10.</p><p><em>He gave the company their Polaris Star.</em></p><p><strong>Everything that's not the main thing is just a hobby or a distraction.</strong></p><p>You can fool yourself --and your uncle at Thanksgiving-- that you can "do it all".</p><p>But you can’t fool history.</p><p>No one ever became the G.O.A.T. by doing it all. Every example of greatness before us started by doing one thing and excelling at it, and only then expanding.</p><p>"But Father Bronques, it's so haaaaard."</p><p>I know. Focusing on one thing is not human nature.</p><p>Our nature is to go towards the path of least resistance.</p><p>Everyone wants to express their "whole self". Whatever that means.</p><p>But the passage to that endpoint is the same for all of those who are your heroes.</p><p><em>You must train yourself to become something specific before becoming the full-fledged person you’re destined to flourish into afterward.</em></p><p>The alternative path?</p><p>Reject discipline... Be “everything” and do everything... And end up resembling every other dull rock on the edge of the beach.</p><p>The choice is yours.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>IF YOU ABSOLUTELY HAVE TO BE A MULTI-HYPHENATE... DO IT THIS WAY</strong></h1><p>You wanna be a multi-hyphenate?</p><p>Jumping between differing art forms? </p><p>That won't work. But if you absolutely NEED to split your brain... Then at least be the kind of multi-hyphenate that will help your cause:</p><p><em>Act like an artist and also be your own manager.</em></p><p>Split your time between making your art and opening doors for yourself.</p><p>But whatever you do... Make one the servant of the other. You can't serve two masters.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>If you respect your art... Make a commitment to ONE thing for the next few years so that the world can conspire to help you achieve it.</strong></h1><p>Decide what your walking towards, and you wake up calm every day and follow it.</p><p>There won't need to be stress in it.</p><p>Because you'll know what you’re doing.</p><p>You'll know where you’re going.</p><h2>YOU'LL BE PULLED TOWARDS YOUR GOALS.</h2><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">You'll stop </span>yearning, and being<span style="color: var(--squid-ink);"> over-emotional about results.</span></p><p><span style="color: var(--squid-ink);">You stop feeling pressured by your lack of attainment. Because you'll always see the target in your mind.</span></p><p><em>Put that one thought in your head at the beginning of the day and hold it there all day at the exclusion of all other thoughts.</em></p><p>The thought you put there becomes your Polaris Star.</p><p>It guides your actions.</p><p>Your life then becomes the result of those movies you chose to play inside your head.</p><p>If you had continued having random thoughts all day, then you'd get a random life.</p><p>But now that you have a "bright and loud re-run of your end goal" playing over and over in your head... It eventually becomes true.</p><p>(Good… Or bad btw)</p><p>No amount of energy spent will get you far if you don’t clearly see where you want to go.</p><p>The clearer you see, the faster you arrive. </p><p>That's why you should choose your thoughts with as much care as you choose what movies you watch, what clothes you wear, and what you have for dinner.</p><p>Before you say, "But, I can't CHOOSE my thoughts. They appear randomly." Let me remind you... Of course they would. Because you’ve never trained your mind to do otherwise. What do you expect?</p><p>Minds need to be trained, just like our bodies do.</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/21444354" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">But it takes practice to focus like a champion. </a></p><p>Decide.</p><p>Right now. </p><p><em>Why should you have anything less?</em></p><h3><strong> "You can have it all. Just not all at once." Oprah</strong></h3><p>===</p><p><em>P.S. If you need help finding your Polaris Star... Email me right now and ask for the 'Polaris Star Formula Emails'-- and I'll hook you up. </em></p><p></p>
</div>

      <div data-original-comment-body="I've identified my audience ( I think!) Like choosing a door to go through but as you do so, it just opens up an even wider selection of pathways..
so as I understand it you would then just keep selecting the path that's a best fit for your Polaris star and audience, a constant checking and selection to stay on course..there is no straight pathway? ( wouldn't that be sooo simple) 
I also have the dilemma of being adept in trad art skills but totally besotted with VR, AR and digital. People resonate with the trad stuff but filter out the new...I guess because they are familiar, mainstream, comfortable...but millions are out there doing it. VR and AR take effort to appreciate if you are unfamiliar. 
So to choose between the skills that bore and don't challenge me and those that excite me in order to communicate the message is tricky. ( bear with me, I'm thinking it through as I write)
I'm focusing on art as a supportive nurturing medium 
if I understand correctly, either I can resort to using trad methods in order to communicate the message....go into their world and gently nudge them into a broader perspective ( does that mean Im possibly focusing on wrong audience/muse) or do I stay true to myself, continue to explore new technologies as an art form and focus on what may well be a very minimal audience?
Which is the most important? communicating the message immediately ( and developing the skills you love in the background) or  developing the skills that excite you..and then using those to communicate the message?

"Let the world pay you for whatever they like. Knowing your focus and having a vision of who you want to become is ALL YOU. Eventually, the world catches up. ❤️"
